description: Pork ribs cut from the upper rib cage near the loin. They are smaller and leaner than spare ribs and are commonly seasoned, slow-cooked, and finished with barbecue sauce or a dry rub.
core_ingredients: Brown sugar or another sweetener for rubs
preparation_steps: Remove or score the membrane if desired and pat the ribs dry.
Season with a dry rub or marinade.
Cook low and slow by smoking, baking, or grilling until tender.
Brush with sauce near the end if making a glazed style.
Rest briefly, slice between bones, and serve.

nutrition.carbohydrates: Low without sauce; higher with sweet sauce

health_advice.benefits: Rich in protein and satisfying as a main dish.
health_advice.risks: Can be high in sodium, saturated fat, and sugar when heavily seasoned or sauced.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose moderate portions, pair with vegetables, and use less sugary sauce if eating it regularly.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally meat-based. It may be gluten-free if the rub and sauce do not contain wheat-based additives.

allergens: Usually none inherent, but sauces may contain gluten, soy, mustard, or celery depending on brand

serving_info.typical_serving: A half rack or several ribs
